title: America's Founding Ideas Coloring Adventure
description: A coloring activity that introduces young students to important symbols and ideas that helped form the United States of America.
instructions: Color each part of the image according to the suggestions or use your own creative colors. As you color, read the facts to learn about America's founding ideas!

content.image: A detailed scene showing the Liberty Bell in the center, with the American flag waving above it. To the left is a simplified version of the Declaration of Independence with a quill pen beside it. To the right is a bald eagle. The background shows Independence Hall with simple outlines.

elements.name: Liberty Bell
elements.description: Color the Liberty Bell brown or bronze. This bell represents freedom and liberty.

elements.name: American Flag
elements.description: Color the stripes red and white, and the star section blue with white stars.

elements.name: Declaration of Independence
elements.description: Color the document light tan or yellow like old paper, with brown or black writing.

elements.name: Bald Eagle
elements.description: Color the eagle's body brown, with a white head and yellow beak.

elements.name: Independence Hall
elements.description: Color the building red-brown with white windows and doors.

learningFacts: The Liberty Bell is a symbol of American independence and freedom.
The American flag's stars represent the states, and the stripes represent the original 13 colonies.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in 1776 and announced that America would be free from British rule.
The bald eagle became America's national bird and represents strength and freedom.
Independence Hall in Philadelphia is where both the Declaration of Independence and the Constitution were debated and signed.
